item.title: Poilievre says he’s offered to go to U.S. to make Canada’s case
item.description: Conservative Leader has been under pressure to explain how he’d manage U.S. trade dispute if elected prime minister

item.title: Canadian military members likely involved in planning U.S. strikes on Iran, retired general says
item.description: A retired Canadian major-general says personnel on exchange with U.S. Central Command were likely involved in planning and targeting airstrikes on Iran unless Ottawa explicitly barred participation. As U.S. and Israeli forces intensify their campaign, The Department of National Defence has so far declined to clarify Canada's operational role.

item.title: Hailing India as a 'natural partner,' Carney says Canada can secure a free trade deal by year's end
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's Indian charm offensive continued over the weekend as he set aside any notion of bilateral bad blood and hailed the South Asian country as a "natural partner."

item.title: Mark Carney walks a tightrope heading into meeting with India’s Narendra Modi
item.description: On Monday, Carney will try to repair relations with his Indian counterpart, who provokes concern and admiration both at home and abroad.

item.title: Mark Carney hails India as ‘natural partner,’ sidestepping questions over alleged violence and political interference in Canada
Mark Carney’s trip to India was unimaginable more than a year ago. Now it’s his Davos speech in action
Carney says Canada won’t be involved militarily in U.S.-Israel action in Iran
Tens of thousands march in Richmond Hill in support of anti-regime movement in Iran after U.S. and Israeli airstrikes
Canada should not have an election before reviewing CUSMA trade deal, says Poilievre
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Polievre says Canada should not have an election before it reviews the Canada-U.S.-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA) this year — and instead he's reiterating his proposal the Carney government set up an all-party committee on the matter.

item.title: Canada poised to become 'one of the largest suppliers of LNG in the world': Energy minister
item.description: Canada's energy minister says the country is poised to become one of the world's biggest suppliers of liquefied natural gas, exporting as much as 100 million tonnes per year.

item.title: The number of Ontarians over 80 is about to surge. Advocates say the Ford government needs a strategy
item.description: Multiple ministries offer piecemeal programs, but the expectation of more than 1.5 million Ontarians occupying the octogenarian-plus cohort by…

item.title: ‘It’s very difficult’: Iranian community fears for loved ones back home as Iran faces internet, phone blackouts after U.S., Israeli airstrikes
Mark Carney backs U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, doesn’t expect Canada to get involved militarily
What’s next for the sprawling ravine site of the shuttered Ontario Science Centre?
item.description: These are some of the ideas percolating for the possible uses of the property.

item.title: This woman was abandoned as a baby in a Toronto washroom. Now she’s telling her story
Carney says Canada supports U.S. action in Iran but won’t get involved militarily
World leaders react to Israeli, U.S. strikes in Iran, Reza Pahlavi calls them ‘humanitarian intervention’
Canada aiming to sign India trade deal this year, Carney says in Mumbai
item.description: MUMBAI - Canada is aiming to sign a comprehensive trade deal with India this year, Prime Minister Mark Carney told a business audience in Mumb…

item.title: Police-involved shooting leaves man dead in Mississauga, officer survives bullet through cap
Ottawa's Iranian diaspora shares hopes, fears in the wake of military strike
item.description: There's a mix of reactions from Iranian Canadians in Ottawa on Saturday in the wake of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes targeting their home country. Some feel the airstrikes are overdue, while some worry war won't bring peace.

item.title: How Doug Ford got pulled into a fight over a Toronto hockey arena and youth centre — and why this faceoff may be ending
item.description: The fight to save Weston Lions Arena has attracted attention of Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, local youth groups — and Ontario's premier.

item.title: How Toronto is bracing for 300,000 World Cup fans — with just about 100 days before kickoff
Canada supports U.S. actions in destroying Iran's nuclear program, Carney says
item.description: As U.S. President Donald Trump charges ahead with a major attack on Iran, Prime Minister Mark Carney says Canada supports at least one component of the American mission: destroying Iran's nuclear program.

item.title: Canadian universities ink partnerships with Indian counterparts during Carney’s visit
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister says pacts will expand research, student and faculty exchanges, AI centres

item.title: U.S. says 3 service members killed since U.S. and Israel launched strikes against Iran
He was hired to fight for Wales's future generations. Does Canada need a job like that?
item.description: For the last three years, Derek Walker has been advocating for the rights of Wales's youngest generations — and generations still to come. As Canadian youth grapple with issues like climate change, affordability and the rise of AI, advocates say it's time the federal government appoints its own champion for generational fairness.

item.title: Canadian passports are about to get more expensive
item.description: The cost of a Canadian passport is about to rise and plans by Prime Minister Mark Carney's government to overhaul the way it sets prices could result in them being even more expensive in the future.

item.title: No exemptions for India on foreign interference concerns, Anand says
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister declines to clarify whether she believes India is still conducting foreign interference in Canada

item.title: Carney backs U.S. air strikes on Iran
item.description: Canadians in Iran should shelter in place, PM says

item.title: Ontario’s plan to increase the powers of special constables drawing mixed reviews
item.description: Video

item.title: Ontario Place, construction and the new Science Centre: Your questions answered
Minister leaves door open to tabling new police search powers bill after 1st attempt faced backlash
item.description: Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangaree is adamant Canada's police and intelligence agencies are getting additional search powers, leaving the door open to introducing a new lawful access bill after his government's first attempt faced backlash.

item.title: AI minister says OpenAI still not doing enough in wake of B.C. shooting, will meet CEO Altman
item.description: Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon says OpenAI's recent commitments to adjust its policies in the wake of the shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., do not go far enough and plans to meet with CEO Sam Altman.

item.title: Solomon to meet OpenAI CEO Altman in wake of mass killings in Tumbler Ridge, B.C.
College of Family Physicians of Canada calls on Ottawa to increase support for family doctors
item.description: The College of Family Physicians of Canada is urging the government to reduce administrative burdens and barriers for family physicians so that they can deliver better patient care.

item.title: Doug Ford unveils design for new Science Centre at Ontario Place
Politics, trade on tap for Poilievre during trip to U.K. and Germany
Canada can’t brush aside security concerns about India, ex-intelligence adviser warns
Carney looks to strengthen economic ties with India on trip to Mumbai, New Delhi
Opposition parties back changes to status rules in Indian Act, Liberals say not yet
Senate seeks time limit on political parties’ privacy law
item.description: Bill C-4 amendment addresses provisions that exempt parties from provincial laws

item.title: Supplying India with uranium ‘very possible in near future,’ Premier Moe says
item.description: Saskatchewan and New Brunswick premiers join Carney on his first visit to India as Prime Minister

item.title: Six storeys, 10 units: On paper, it seemed the kind of housing Toronto wants. Then its Scarborough neighbours weighed in
item.description: Mayor Olivia Chow is looking to reform the committee of adjustment after it rejected a building the developer says conforms to the city's new …

item.title: Canada's economy contracted unexpectedly in fourth quarter of 2025
item.description: Gross domestic product contracted ​at an annualized pace of 0.6 per cent in the quarter running from October to December, ​Statistics Canada said. The country's overall growth for 2025 was 1.7 per cent — in line with Bank of Canada expectations, but the slowest pace of annual growth since ​the decline in 2020.

item.title: China suspending some agricultural tariffs on Canada starting March 1
item.description: China said on Friday it would suspend some tariffs on Canadian agricultural products imposed during a trade spat between Beijing and Ottawa, after Canadian Prime Minister Mark ‌Carney struck an initial deal with Beijing during a visit in January.

item.title: Indigenous Peoples play a key role in national security: Gov. Gen. Mary Simon
item.description: OTTAWA - Gov. Gen. Mary Simon says Indigenous communities across the country — and particularly in the Arctic — play a vital role in Canada's …

item.title: Federal government reports deficit of $26.1B for its April-to-December period
Doug Ford blasts the ‘radical left’ at Toronto city hall over estimate on cost of replacing speed cameras
Energy-hungry India tells Carney 'we are willing to buy whatever Canada is offering'
item.description: India wants to buy any energy product it can from Canada and its officials are urging the federal government to streamline approvals for various projects so it can tap into new supplies to feed a rapidly growing country with relatively few natural resources of its own.

item.title: Poilievre makes the pivot on Trump, but repudiates Carney’s foreign policy
item.description: Conservative Leader called out the U.S. President, but didn’t go far enough to match the Canadian mood

item.title: Liberal budget bill passes House of Commons after months of debate
item.description: The more-than-600-page bill now moves to the Senate

item.title: Politics Insider: Alberta projects $9.37-billion deficit as spending expected to reach record high
item.description: And, Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre says Canada should not declare a ‘permanent rupture’ with the U.S.

item.title: Tumbler Ridge shooter had 2nd ChatGPT account despite being banned, OpenAI says
item.description: OpenAI says the company found a second ChatGPT account belonging to the Tumbler Ridge, B.C., shooter when her name was made public — after a previous account was banned in June for posts about gun violence.

item.title: Alberta budget 2026 comes with spending hikes but $9.4B deficit
item.description: Premier Danielle Smith’s UCP government has introduced a new Alberta budget that promises more money for health and education but also an eye-popping deficit of $9.4 billion.

item.title: Liberals survive confidence vote as House passes budget implementation bill
item.description: The legislation enacts key measures proposed in the Liberals' 2025 federal budget, which passed a confidence vote for Prime Minister Mark Carney's government in November last year.

item.title: Poilievre calls Trump’s Canada attacks ‘wrong,’ urges U.S. trade stability
item.description: Poilievre called for the creation of an all-party working group on the Canada-U.S.-Mexico Agreement on trade as the three countries begin a review of the deal.

item.title: Liberal, Bloc Québécois rematch in Terrebonne riding after Supreme Court nixes result
item.description: Bloc Leader Yves-François Blanchet announced his candidate Thursday, paying tribute to Sinclair-Desgagné's relentless drive.

item.title: Poilievre pitches critical-mineral stockpile as leverage in trade talks with the U.S.
item.description: The postPoilievre pitches critical-mineral stockpile as leverage in trade talks with the U.S.appeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Foreign influence commissioner could monitor an Alberta referendum on separation
item.description: Americans seeking to influence a possible Alberta referendum on separating from Canada could have to register as foreign agents, says Canada's new foreign influence watchdog.

item.title: PM urged to clarify whether he believes India still carrying out interference in Canada
item.description: A senior government official previously told reporters Ottawa does not believe India is still meddling in Canadian politics and orchestrating violence

item.title: Ontario reveals design for new science centre at Toronto’s waterfront, says it could be ready in 3 years
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford pulled back the curtain Thursday on the province’s design for a new science centre at Toronto’s waterfront, calling it a “world-class” facility that will be “jaw-dropping.”

item.title: Doug Ford takes issue with Toronto's 13-year timeline to build road safety measures
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford took issue Thursday with a City of Toronto report that concluded it would take upwards of 13 years and cost $52 million to build speed bumps and other traffic calming measures in school zones.

item.title: A federal program to provide health care for refugees is — once again — sparking a fiery debate
item.description: The stated concerns about the Interim Federal Health Program and the political rhetoric harken back to the last time changes were made to the program — an episode that ended in a rebuke from the Federal Court.

item.title: Foreign influence registry will make bad actors ‘think twice,’ nominee says
item.description: If confirmed by Parliament, Anton Boegman would oversee Canada's new and long-delayed foreign influence transparency registry and administer penalties for non-compliance.

item.title: Poilievre targets Trump, urges Canada to focus on what it can control in major speech
item.description: In a speech laying out the details of how Canada should respond to the Trump administration, Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre directly attacked U.S. President Donald Trump's trade and tariff policies, urging Canadians to focus on what they can control.

item.title: Conservative MP searches for ‘antifa’ in federal government, Canadian Armed Forces
item.description: Federal departments and the Canadian Armed Forces must answer by April how many members of anti-fascist groups work for the public service and the military.

item.title: Federal government commits $1.55-billion for First Nations children
item.description: Ottawa will extend Jordan’s Principle, which provides health and education support, for one year

item.title: Métis artifact from 1920s repatriated from Vatican
item.description: A model dog sled was one of 62 items repatriated after decades of calls for their return

item.title: ‘They eat your tires’: Toronto pothole claims jump 50%, while number of repairs see sharp drop
Canadians on Mexican trade mission undeterred by drug violence
item.description: The postCanadians on Mexican trade mission undeterred by drug violenceappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Alberta to present 2026 budget that includes a multibillion-dollar deficit
item.description: Smith says the main culprit is low oil prices but has also pointed to the costs associated with sharp population growth

item.title: Giorgio Mammoliti has died. Here are 8 times he turned Toronto politics into performance art
Fighter jets and subs exempt from new defence industrial strategy, feds say
item.description: The postFighter jets and subs exempt from new defence industrial strategy, feds sayappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: After a low point in relations, Carney heads to India looking for a reset
item.description: Canada is behind its peers in preferential trade access to the country. The Prime Minister is hoping to change that

item.title: After decades of mistrust and missteps, where do India and Canada fit together?
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney wants to reset the relationship on his trip

item.title: Giorgio Mammoliti, former Toronto councillor and Rob Ford ally, dies at 64: ‘He was a real streetfighter’
India no longer seen as conducting foreign interference in Canada, senior official says
item.description: The statement, made days before Prime Minister Mark Carney is set to travel to the country, marks a significant change from 2024, when relations hit a low

item.title: Liberals move forward with nominations as election talk ramps up
item.description: Sources, including three MPs, say the preparations do not mean a snap election is imminent

item.title: Poilievre to fly to UK, Germany on first international trip as Opposition leader
item.description: As Prime Minister Mark Carney heads to India, Australia, and Japan, his chief political opponent Pierre Poilievre is bound for the UK and Germany.

item.title: CSIS acting to prevent possible Iranian proxy attacks on U.S. targets in Canada, former spies say
item.description: Iran has threatened retaliation over potential U.S. action, raising concerns its proxies may strike U.S. targets in Canada and elsewhere

item.title: ‘I hope they hurt’: Unifor irate over early idling of Crown Royal factory in Amherstburg, Ont.
item.description: Unifor is upset over the early idling of the Crown Royal factory in Amherstburg, Ont.

item.title: Ex-Raptor Porter signs with Seattle USBL team
item.description: Former Toronto Raptor Jontay Porter has signed with the Seattle SuperHawks for the 2026 season of the United States Basketball League, a minor pro league relaunching this spring with eight teams.

item.title: Minister ‘disappointed’ in OpenAI, but why is AI regulation taking years?
item.description: Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon said he is giving OpenAI a chance to quickly detail 'concrete' actions before the government addresses the issue through legislation.

item.title: Feds threaten legislation unless OpenAI makes changes following B.C. killings
item.description: The postFeds threaten legislation unless OpenAI makes changes following B.C. killingsappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Toronto councillors grilled the CEO of Circular Materials on its troubled recycling rollout. Here’s what he said
Canada to send $8-million in food aid for Cuba as fuel crisis continues
item.description: Island lost its main source of fuel in January when the U.S. took control of Venezuela’s oil reserves

item.title: The push to create a Canadian rival to GPS
item.description: The postThe push to create a Canadian rival to GPSappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Black trustees concerned Ontario school board supervision weakening anti-racism fight
item.description: Provincial school board takeovers in Ontario are weakening the governance needed to track and end anti-Black racism in schools, Black trustees are warning.

item.title: Dozens of Ontario nursing homes competed for a spot in culture-change pilot project
New Liberal MP Matt Jeneroux to join Carney on India, Australia, Japan trip
item.description: The Prime Minister's Office said Jeneroux will be part of Carney's delegation in his new role as 'special advisor on economic and security partnerships' during the nine-day trip.
